conflicts.name: American Revolutionary War
conflicts.authDetail: Pre-Constitution. Continental Congress authorized.

conflicts.name: Quasi-War with France
conflicts.authDetail: Never declared by Congress. Adams acted on executive authority.

conflicts.name: First Barbary War
conflicts.authDetail: Congress authorized use of force (1802).

conflicts.name: War of 1812
conflicts.authDetail: Declared by Congress June 18, 1812.

conflicts.name: Mexican-American War
conflicts.authDetail: Declared by Congress May 13, 1846.

conflicts.name: American Civil War
conflicts.authDetail: Lincoln acted on executive authority to suppress rebellion. Congress authorized military expansion.

conflicts.name: Spanish-American War
conflicts.authDetail: Declared by Congress April 25, 1898.

conflicts.name: Philippine-American War
conflicts.authDetail: No congressional declaration. Executive action under McKinley.

conflicts.name: World War I
conflicts.authDetail: Declared by Congress April 6, 1917.

conflicts.name: World War II
conflicts.authDetail: Declared by Congress December 8, 1941 (Japan), December 11, 1941 (Germany/Italy).

conflicts.name: Korean War
conflicts.authDetail: Never declared by Congress. Truman called it a "police action" under UN authority.

conflicts.name: Iranian Coup (Operation Ajax)
conflicts.authDetail: Covert CIA operation. No congressional knowledge.

conflicts.name: Guatemalan Coup
conflicts.authDetail: Covert CIA operation (Operation PBSUCCESS).

conflicts.name: Vietnam War
conflicts.authDetail: Gulf of Tonkin Resolution (1964) — later revealed to be based on fabricated intelligence. Never a formal declaration of war.

conflicts.name: Bay of Pigs Invasion
conflicts.authDetail: Covert CIA operation. No congressional authorization or knowledge.

conflicts.name: Dominican Republic Intervention
conflicts.authDetail: Executive action by Johnson. No congressional authorization.

conflicts.name: Chilean Coup Support
conflicts.authDetail: Covert CIA operation. Nixon/Kissinger directive.

conflicts.name: Invasion of Grenada
conflicts.authDetail: Reagan invoked executive authority. UN General Assembly condemned invasion 108-9.

conflicts.name: Invasion of Panama
conflicts.authDetail: Executive action by Bush. No congressional authorization.

conflicts.name: Gulf War (Desert Storm)
conflicts.authDetail: Congress authorized use of force January 12, 1991.

conflicts.name: Somalia Intervention
conflicts.authDetail: UN peacekeeping mission. Executive action by Bush and Clinton.

conflicts.name: Bosnia Intervention
conflicts.authDetail: NATO authority. Clinton did not seek congressional authorization for bombing campaign.

conflicts.name: Kosovo War (NATO Bombing)
conflicts.authDetail: No congressional authorization. Clinton bypassed War Powers Resolution. Congress voted down authorization but also voted down defunding.

conflicts.name: War in Afghanistan
conflicts.authDetail: AUMF passed September 14, 2001. Later used to justify operations in 22 countries.

conflicts.name: Global War on Terror (Other Operations)
conflicts.authDetail: All conducted under the 2001 AUMF — a 60-word authorization written in 3 days that has been used to justify military operations in at least 22 countries.

conflicts.name: Iraq War
conflicts.authDetail: Authorization for Use of Military Force Against Iraq Resolution (2002). Based on false WMD intelligence.

conflicts.name: Global Drone Campaign
conflicts.authDetail: Justified under 2001 AUMF. No specific authorization for most target countries.

conflicts.name: Somalia (AFRICOM Operations)
conflicts.authDetail: Conducted under 2001 AUMF — stretched to cover al-Shabaab despite no connection to 9/11.

conflicts.name: Libya Intervention
conflicts.authDetail: Obama did not seek congressional authorization. Justified under NATO/UN authority. Exceeded 60-day War Powers Act limit.

conflicts.name: Niger & Sahel Operations
conflicts.authDetail: Conducted under 2001 AUMF. Most Americans unaware US troops were in Niger until the 2017 ambush.

conflicts.name: Syrian Civil War Intervention
conflicts.authDetail: Justified under 2001 AUMF — written to target al-Qaeda, applied to ISIS 13 years later.

conflicts.name: War Against ISIS
conflicts.authDetail: Obama used 2001 AUMF (designed for 9/11 attackers) to justify war against ISIS — a group that didn't exist in 2001 and was actually fighting al-Qaeda.

conflicts.name: Yemen War (Saudi Support)
conflicts.authDetail: No congressional authorization. Congress voted to end support in 2019; Trump vetoed. Biden pledged to end but continued.

conflicts.name: Ukraine Military Support
conflicts.authDetail: Congress approved multiple supplemental funding packages. Bipartisan support initially; grew contentious by 2024.

conflicts.name: Red Sea / Houthi Campaign
conflicts.authDetail: No congressional authorization. Biden administration cited Article II self-defense authority. Bipartisan criticism from both parties.

conflicts.name: Iran War (2026)
conflicts.authDetail: No congressional authorization sought. Trump cited executive authority. Bipartisan concerns about escalation.
